title: Quiz: Error-Aware Training
description: Test your understanding of training QML models with noise awareness

questions.question: What is the main goal of error-aware training?

options.text: Train models that remain accurate despite noise

questions.explanation: Error-aware training aims to build noise resilience into the model during training, so it performs well despite hardware noise.

questions.question: Why is gradient regularization useful for noisy training?

options.text: It penalizes large gradients that indicate noise sensitivity
It eliminates the need for gradients

questions.explanation: Large gradients mean the loss is sensitive to small parameter changes, which also means sensitivity to noise. Regularization promotes smoother loss landscapes.

questions.question: What is the principle behind noise injection training?

options.text: Add noise to make training faster
Train with intentional noise so the model learns to be robust
Remove noise from the training data

questions.explanation: By training with intentionally added noise, the model learns parameters that work well even when noise is present.

questions.question: Shallower quantum circuits generally accumulate less noise than deeper circuits.
questions.explanation: True. Fewer gates and shorter circuit execution time mean less opportunity for errors to accumulate from decoherence and gate imperfections.

questions.question: What does a noise-margin loss function do?

options.text: Only penalizes errors beyond an expected noise threshold

questions.explanation: Noise-margin loss accepts predictions within the expected noise range and only penalizes deviations beyond that margin.

questions.question: How does variance-aware training adapt to shot noise?

options.text: Uses more shots for every measurement
Weights gradient contributions by inverse variance

questions.explanation: Variance-aware training gives more weight to gradient estimates with lower variance, making updates more reliable.

questions.question: What is hardware-efficient ansatz design?

options.text: Using the native gate set of the specific quantum hardware

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansatz uses gates native to the hardware to minimize decomposition overhead and associated errors.

questions.question: Which are benefits of integrating error mitigation into training? (Select all)

options.text: Gradients are computed on mitigated values
Model learns to work with mitigation
Parameters are optimized for mitigated performance

questions.explanation: Integrated mitigation provides mitigated gradients, co-adapts the model with mitigation, and optimizes for mitigated outputs. However, it increases per-iteration cost, not speed.
